title: Phenol/chloroform extraction of DNA from cyanobacteria
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bhnsb","text":"Phenol/chloroform-based DNA extraction from cells pre-treated with RNase A, lysozyme, proteinase K, and SDS. The protocol was optimized for extracting DNA from Microcystis aeruginosa, but works well for other cyanobacteria.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":160,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Transfer aqueous (top) layer to clean Eppendorf tube without removing any of the organic layer.
Add 25 µL of 100 mg/mL lysozyme for a final concentration of 5 mg/mL lysozyme. undetermined, of 100 mg/mL lysozyme

Repeat steps 10, 11 and 12 twice (a total of 3 phenol extractions) or until no visible protein layer is seen. Each time, remove the last of the aqueous layer along with some of the organic layer and add to the 're-extraction' Eppendorf tube (refer to step 14). go to step #10 Protein layer will be white scum at interface. go to step #14
Add 50 µL of 100 µg/mL RNase A TER buffer (RNase A plus TE buffer) for a final concentration of 10 µg/mL RNase A. undetermined, RNase A TER buffer
Resuspend pellet in 425 µL of standard TE buffer. undetermined, TE buffer Standard TE buffer -10 mM Tris -1 mM EDTA -pH 8
Add 250 mL chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (24:1), mix gently and completely. undetermined, chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (24:1)
Add 250 mL phenol (pH 8.0). Mix gently and completely. undetermined, phenol (pH 8.0)
Add 50 µL of 10% sodium dodecyl sulfate. undetermined, 10% sodium dodecyl sulfate
Add 50 µL of 1 mg/mL proteinase K for a final concentration of 100 µg/mL proteinase K.
Repeat steps 16-18 to remove all phenol. (2/2) go to step #16
Transfer aqueous (top) layer to clean Eppendorf tube without removing any of the organic layer.(1/2)
Add 500 µL of chloroform and mix gently.(1/2) undetermined, chloroform
Add 0.1 volume of 3 M sodium acetate to the collected aqueous phase and mix gently.
Extract the mixed aqueous/organic contents of the 're-extraction' Eppendorf tube and follow extraction steps 10-19. Combine the final aqueous layer with that collected above. go to step #10
Add 2 volumes of ice cold 100% ethanol and gently mix well. Strings of precipitating DNA should become visible.
Place tube in -80 °C freezer until ethanol mixture is partially frozen (~1 hr.) Undetermined, freezer 01:00:00, approximate time for partial ethanol freeze
Spin at maximum speed in benchtop centrifuge at 4 °C for 30 min. 00:30:00, centrifugation Undetermined, centrifugation
Wash the DNA pellet by adding 500 µL of ice-cold 70% ethanol to the tube and pipetting it gently, several times. undetermined, ice-cold 70% ethanol
Spin at maximum speed in a benchtop centrifuge at 4 °C for 15 min. 00:15:00, centrifugation Undetermined, centrifugation

Place in a heater block at ~37 °C for less than 5 minutes to evaporate all ethanol. Be careful! DO NOT dry to completion ! Alternatively, you can air dry to remove the ethanol. 00:04:59, ethanol drying Undetermined, heater block
Re-suspend the DNA pellet in 50-100 µL of TE buffer or water (depending on downstream use and needed concentration). undetermined, TE buffer or water
Concentrate ~4 mL of cellular culture in an Eppendorf tube by centrifugation at 10,000 xg for ~5 min. undetermined, cellular culture 00:05:00, centrifugation at 10,000 xg
Discard supernatant.
Incubate at 37 °C for 20 minutes. Undetermined, incubation 00:20:00, incubation
Incubate at 50 °C for 2 hr. Undetermined, incubation 02:00:00, incubation
Spin at maximum speed in benchtop centrifuge for at least 2 minutes. Longer spin times, (up to 10 min.) may help in separating the phases.

Remove the last of the aqueous layer along with some of the organic layer (to ensure all aqueous volume is collected) and add it to a new, clean Eppendorf tube for later re-extraction. This re-extraction will significantly increase DNA recovery.
Spin at maximum speed in benchtop centrifuge for at least 2 minutes. (1/2) This will help remove traces of phenol. 00:02:00, centrifugation
Discard supernatant and very carefully aspirate the remaining droplets of liquid from the tube without disrupting the DNA pellet.
Discard supernatant and carefully aspirate the remaining droplets of liquid from the tube without disrupting the DNA pellet.
Freeze at -20 or -80 °C.
creator: Robbie Martin
